title: My Gut Speaks. I Am the One Who Listens.
subtitle: An affirmation for trusting your own inner knowing over external authority
excerpt: A short affirmation for reclaiming the relationship with your own intuition. For the habit of waiting for external permission or confirmation before trusting what is already clear inside.

practice.whenToUse: Use as a morning affirmation to set the day's relationship with your own knowing, or before a decision where your gut sense is present but being drowned out.

content.text: A short affirmation for reclaiming the authority of your own inner knowing. Many women are more practised at consulting external sources (other people's opinions, logic, what is expected) than at attending to what they already sense. This affirmation is about the relationship between the knowing and the one responsible for listening to it.
Say it standing, hand on your belly, three times slowly:
My gut speaks. I am the one who listens. I trust what I hear.

Three sentences. The first establishes that the gut does speak: it is already active, already sending. The second makes you the receiver, not a bystander. The third is the act of trust: taking what arrives seriously.
